data.title: SNDK or QCOM: Morgan Stanley Picks One Top AI Semiconductor Stock to Buy
data.description:
AI adoption is accelerating across data centers, cloud platforms, and enterprise systems, but one part of the hardware stack is becoming a real constraint – memory chips. Advanced AI workloads depend heavily on high-performance memory to store, move, and process enormous datasets at high speed. Training and running large models is not just about compute anymore; it is equally about how fast memory is available and how efficiently it can be accessed.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That demand is now rising faster than supply. High-bandwidth and advanced DRAM production requires specialized manufacturing capacity, long build-out cycles, and complex packaging, which limits how quickly output can expand. The result is a tightening memory market and upward pricing pressure in certain segments.

These constraints do not affect every part of the tech sector equally. Hardware makers that rely on memory components – including PC and smartphone vendors – can face higher input costs and potential supply bottlenecks. By contrast, companies directly focused on memory production or closely tied to high-end memory demand may benefit from stronger pricing power and improved margins. Other semiconductor firms sit somewhere in between, depending on their product mix and customer exposure.

Against this backdrop, Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore, who ranks among the top 2% of analysts on TipRanks, draws a clear distinction between companies leveraged to memory trends and those more exposed to end-device demand. Applying that framework to SanDisk (NASDAQ:SNDK) and Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM), Moore comes down in favor of only one, while taking a more cautious stance on the other. His reasoning, and what it implies for investors, is worth a closer look.

SanDisk

Let's start with SanDisk. This company is probably familiar to you – especially if you make regular use of portable USB drives. SanDisk is a leader in that market; its handy little thumb drives, with the company logo on them, are easy to find and convenient to use to keep files and information portable. SanDisk has been in operation since 1988, was traded on Wall Street from 1995 to 2016, and was owned by Western Digital from 2016 until 2025. It spun off from Western Digital last February and now trades on the NASDAQ index.

SanDisk's most public face might be thumb drives, but the company is well-known as an important supplier of higher-end memory, too. The company sells both internal and external solid-state drives (SSDs) and offers data storage solutions based on NAND flash technology. These solutions have found favor in data center applications and in edge devices. SanDisk operates globally, boasting more than 10,000 employees and a footprint in more than 30 countries. The company is proactive about protecting its intellectual property rights and holds over 11,000 patents worldwide.

In recent months, SanDisk's strong position as a high-end memory provider has provided powerful support for the stock. Shares in SNDK are up over 1,600% in the past 12 months and 155% just year-to-date.

These gains have been driven by data center demand, which, in fiscal period 2Q26, the last reported, was up 64% sequentially and 76% year-over-year. The data center segment brought in $440 million in revenue for SanDisk during fiscal 2Q.

The company reported strong results in other parts of that report, with total revenue at $3.3 billion, up 61% year-over-year and coming in $337 million better than had been anticipated. SanDisk's $6.20 non-GAAP EPS popped an impressive 404% year-over-year and beat the forecast by $2.66 per share. SanDisk ended the fiscal quarter with $1.54 billion available in cash and liquid assets.

In his coverage of SanDisk, Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore takes a constructive view of SanDisk and argues that the company is operating from a position of real strength rather than a short-lived upswing.

"Earnings are above the long term trend, but based on our data points it seems likely to stay that way for more than one year – really for as long as the AI trajectory remains this robust. Enterprise NAND demand is every bit as inelastic as DRAM, and while there are likely to be some headwinds in consumer because of price increases, the supply picture is also really positive – capital spending remains at low levels, amid reports that some NAND cleanroom space may even be allocated to DRAM. That points to a long runway for pricing increases and for EPS revisions," Moore noted.

The top-rated analyst goes on to say what this means for investors, in quantitative terms, writing: "At what is still a single-digit P/E multiple on peak earnings power potential—and roughly a 9x multiple on our new CY26 EPS we think there's more upside here as long as the data-center build continues unabated. And while earnings are above our view of long term earnings power, which is our basis for valuation, the level of peak earnings is a major part of the through cycle formula – and they get to keep the cash flow they generate along the way. With earnings crossing the $80 run rate, and no end in sight in the intermediate term, we are moving our through cycle earnings power estimate to $30."

Moore finishes by rating SNDK as Overweight (i.e., Buy), with a $690 price target that implies an upside of 13% for the next 12 months. (To watch Moore's track record, click here)

Overall, SanDisk holds a Moderate Buy consensus rating on the Street, based on 15 recent analyst reviews that break down to 11 Buys and 4 Holds. (See SNDK stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Rivian Stock (RIVN) Skyrockets as Five-Star Analysts Weigh in on Q4 Results
data.description:
EV maker Rivian (RIVN) is surging in today's trading following its fourth-quarter earnings results. Interestingly, Automotive revenue and gross profit were still under pressure, mainly because deliveries declined following changes to U.S. EV incentives and because average selling prices were lower. However, the bigger picture showed progress. In 2025, Rivian generated about $144 million in gross profit compared to a $1.2 billion loss in 2024. This sharp improvement was driven largely by fast growth in its higher-margin software and services segment, along with better profitability on each vehicle sold.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Looking ahead to 2026, the company expects deliveries to increase to a range of 62,000 to 67,000 vehicles. Management described 2026 as a year that will be focused on investing and scaling production as it prepares to ramp up its R2 and midsize platforms. As a result, Analyst reactions were mixed but leaned slightly optimistic. For instance, Wedbush kept its Buy rating and $25 price target, with five-star analyst Dan Ives saying he remains confident in Rivian's long-term strategy as it optimizes R1 production and prepares for its next growth phase.

Separately, Needham called 2026 a transitional year. It noted that while higher spending on autonomy research and new launches is weighing on near-term results, improving vehicle economics and strong software growth tied to the Volkswagen (VWAGY) joint venture support future margin expansion. On the other hand, Mizuho, led by five-star-rated Vijay Rakesh, maintained its Sell rating by arguing that the broader EV sector continues to face significant challenges.

Is RIVN Stock a Buy or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on RIVN stock based on nine Buys, eight Holds, and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average RIVN price target of $18.14 per share implies 6.6% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Bank of America (BAC) Says U.S. Treasuries Are the Best 'Safe Haven' for Investors
data.description:
Bank of America (BAC) says investors should buy U.S. Treasuries during the current stock market volatility as they are the best "safe haven" asset.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bank of America's Chief Investment Strategist Michael Hartnett recommends investors purchase 30-year Treasurys, as the U.S. government won't let the long bond yield go above 5%. Hartnett argues that the 30-year Treasury is currently the best hedge against market volatility.

The bank executive's latest views on the long bond were published in the weekly "Flow Show" note that came out between strong payrolls data and a benign inflation report this week. In the note, Hartnett says that the U.S. Federal Reserve might be done lowering interest rates for the foreseeable future.

Bank of America's Counterintuitive View

If Hartnett is accurate about interest rates, it would imply that the U.S. yield curve will steepen, with interest rates rising faster at the longer end of the curve than the shorter end, benefitting the 30-year Treasury.

The idea that the U.S. central bank is done cutting rates runs counter to the prevailing view on Wall Street, where futures markets have at least two rate cuts penciled in for this year. Also, most analysts are recommending investors seek safety in assets such as gold and blue-chip stocks rather than U.S. government bonds.

Regardless, Hartnett writes that the current set-up for the 30-year Treasury looks attractive.

Is BAC Stock a Buy?

Bank of America's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 19 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 15 Buy and four Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average BAC price target of $61.64 implies 17.59% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Fed's Miran is 'Unconcerned about Inflation,' Urges Rate Cuts
data.description:
Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran reiterated his stance on lowering interest rates, arguing that a strong jobs report for January doesn't weaken the case for easing monetary policy while inflation remains under control.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"As long as I remain unconcerned about inflation, I think it makes sense to continue trying to underwrite the labor market with looser monetary policy, particularly as supply expands more than demand and the economy can grow without generating inflation," Miran said at a Dallas Fed event.

Inflation Slows Ahead of March FOMC Meeting

Miran's statement was put to the test when the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) published January's Consumer Price Index (CPI) this morning, showing inflation rising by 2.4% annually. That marked the lowest rate of growth since May 2025. In addition, core CPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, increased by 2.5% from the prior year, registering its lowest level since March 2021.

Miran has voted in favor of rate cuts in every Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting since he joined the board in September. The next meeting is scheduled for March 18.

data.title: Shareholders Sue Fermi after Seeing Red over Project Matador Claims
data.description:
A class action lawsuit was filed against Fermi (FRMI) on January 5, 2026.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The federal securities class action alleges that plaintiffs acquired Fermi stock: pursuant and/or traceable to the Registration Statement (as defined in the complaint) issued in connection with the Company's October 2025 initial public offering ("IPO" or the "Offering"); and/or between October 1, 2025 and December 11, 2025, also known as the Class Period.

Plaintiffs are now seeking compensation for financial losses incurred upon public revelation of the company's alleged negligence or misconduct during those times.

What Does Fermi Do?

Fermi does business as an energy and artificial intelligence ("AI") infrastructure company.

As such, it claims that it is revolutionizing the expansion of next-generation private grids that deliver highly redundant power at gigawatt scale.

The complaint provides some additional insight into Fermi's purported business,  explaining that Fermi is set up as an REIT and that its  purported goal is to  "build multiple, large scale nuclear reactors and create its own network of large, grid-independent data centers." As also stated in the complaint, these data centers would purportedly be powered through a combination of nuclear, natural gas, solar, and battery energy" to facilitate further development of AI.

The company's first project, Project Matador, purportedly calls for the creation of "the world's largest private energy campus, providing dedicated power for AI workloads." To learn whether you may be eligible for a recovery under this class action, click here.

Why are Shareholders Angry with Fermi?

The complaint alleges negligent preparation of the Registration Statement. As a result, it contains untrue statements of important facts, but lacks corrective statements and disclosures required by law.

The company and two of its senior officers  (the "Individual Defendants") are also accused of deceiving investors by lying and withholding important information about Fermi's business practices and prospects during the Class Period.

In particular, they are accused of omitting truthful information about tenant demand for its Project Matador campus, and ancillary issues, from SEC filings and related material. By knowingly or recklessly doing so, they allegedly caused Fermi stock to trade at artificially inflated prices during the time in question.

The truth came out, according to the shareholders, before the market opened on December 12, 2025. That's when Fermi issued a press release announcing that "the First Tenant notified the Company that it is terminating the AICA, but the parties continue to negotiate the terms of a lease agreement at Project Matador pursuant to the letter of intent."

Let's Take a Closer Look

As alleged, the company and/or Individual Defendants repeatedly made false and misleading public statements throughout the Class Period.

In a shareholder letter issued at the beginning of the Class Period, for example, the company stated in pertinent part: "In November, the parties entered into a $150 million Advance in Aid of Construction Agreement (see Recent Developments). Which establishes  a cost reimbursement framework under which the tenant will fund a portion of  shared infrastructure and utility systems in advance of occupancy."

In the same letter, Fermi also stated in relevant part: "The AIAC establishes a cost reimbursement framework under which the tenant will fund a portion of shared infrastructure and utility systems in advance of occupancy. This agreement marks a pivotal milestone in Fermi's commercial program, strengthening alignment with the prospective tenant and advancing the parties toward execution of a long-term campus lease."

Lastly, in a quarterly report filed with the SEC on November 12, 2025, Fermi stated: "We expect a significant portion of our contracted revenue base to come from investment-grade tenants, many of whom are anticipated to provide upfront capital contributions or structured prepayments to support dedicated infrastructure buildout."

Actions You Can Take

If you have purchased the company's stock during the Class Period,  or based on or pursuant to the Registration Statement, you may join the class action as a lead plaintiff, remain a passive class member, or opt out of this litigation and pursue individual claims that may not be available to the class as a whole. To learn more about your options, click here.

The deadline to file for lead plaintiff in this class action is March 6, 2026.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Coinbase Stock Shoots Up 15% even as Analysts Slash COIN Price Forecasts after Q4 Miss
data.description:
Coinbase stock (COIN) is shaking off a rough earnings report. Even though the company reported its first quarterly loss in two years, the share price surged 15.8% during the first hour of trading. This sharp climb happened even as a quiet crypto market forced the exchange to watch its trading volumes slide and its token holdings lose value. While the financials look messy on paper, investors seem to care more about the company's plan to return billions of dollars in cash to shareholders.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Analysts Lower the Ceiling, but Remain Bullish on COIN Stock

Major Wall Street banks were quick to update their math after the report came out Thursday evening. JPMorgan's Kenneth Worthington kept its "overweight" rating but lowered its price target to $252 from $290, noting that users are shifting toward lower-fee trading tools. Canaccord's Joseph Vafi made an even bigger cut, dropping its target from $400 down to $300. These experts are watching the "take rate," the slice of each trade Coinbase keeps as profit, which is getting thinner as more people sign up for the Coinbase One subscription instead of paying per trade.

Coinbase Experienced a Massive Loss

The actual numbers for the end of 2025 were undeniably tough. Coinbase swung to a net loss of $666.7 million, a total reversal from the $1.3 billion profit it saw during the crypto boom a year earlier. Total revenue fell about 20% to $1.78 billion, coming in lower than what Wall Street expected. The company also took a $718 million hit just because the value of the Bitcoin and Ethereum it holds in its own vault dropped during the market slump.

Why COIN Stock Is Surging Anyway

The reason for today's double-digit jump comes down to a few key moves by the company:

  • Massive Buybacks: Coinbase has already spent $1.7 billion buying back its own stock recently to help keep the price steady.
  • Dry Powder for Growth: The Board just approved an additional $2 billion for even more buybacks, signaling they think the stock is currently a steal.
  • New Revenue Streams: The company is successfully moving into prediction markets and stock trading, which helps them make money even when Bitcoin isn't doing much.
  • Resilient Buyers: CFO Alesia Haas mentioned that while prices are volatile, their customers have actually been net buyers during this latest price drop.

Key Takeaway

Essentially, the market is choosing to ignore the ugly numbers from last year. Yes, the company lost a lot of money because Bitcoin fell, but investors are focused on the fact that Coinbase is sitting on a massive pile of cash, over $11 billion. The company is showing a huge amount of confidence through its decision to use that money to buy back its own shares. If they can keep expanding into things like stocks and prediction markets, they might finally stop being a stock that only goes up and down with the price of Bitcoin.

Is Coinbase a Good Stock to Buy?

Coinbase stock (COIN) has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 23 Wall Street analysts. This rating is based on 14 Buy, seven Hold, and two Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average 12-month COIN price target of $275 implies 69.5% upside from current levels. These ratings could continue to change over the next few days.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: AC Earnings: Air Canada Stock Rises as Carrier Returns to Profitability
data.description:
Air Canada's (TSE:AC) stock is up 5% after the company returned to profitability in the fourth quarter of 2025.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Montreal-based airline, which is Canada's flagship carrier, reported C$296 million in net income during Q4 2025, up from a loss of C$644 million a year earlier. Air Canada announced earnings per share of C$0.47, which beat the consensus estimate among analysts of C$0.20.

Revenue in the final three months of last year amounted to C$5.8 billion, up 7% from C$5.4 billion a year ago. Analysts had expected Air Canada's revenue for Q4 2025 to be in a range of C$5.51 billion to C$5.56 billion. The company has struggled since the COVID-19 pandemic struck in 2020.

Aircraft Acquisition

Air Canada CEO Michael Rousseau praised the results, saying that the carrier managed a return to profitability despite shifting travel demands among consumers and amid macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainty.

Along with its latest financial results, Air Canada announced the purchase of eight new Airbus (EADSY) A350-1000 wide-body aircraft, with rights to purchase an additional eight planes at a future date. In recent days, the airline suspended flights to Cuba due to a fuel shortage in the Caribbean nation.

Is AC Stock a Buy?

Air Canada's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among eight Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on five Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average AC price target of C$23.32 implies 15.67% upside from current levels. These ratings could change after the company's financial results.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Trump Plans to Scale Back Steel, Aluminum Tariffs
data.description:
Last June, President Trump imposed a 50% tariff on steel and aluminum imports. However, aluminum prices have soared by 24% since then, while steel has risen about 4%, heightening an affordability crisis faced by many Americans.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Now, the Trump administration is planning to dial back these tariffs, starting with a duty exemption on some steel and aluminum products, according to the Financial Times. In addition, the administration will conduct narrower national security investigations into specific products that could face tariffs, a move that contrasts with its previous sweeping approach.

Americans Pay for 90% of Trump's Tariffs, Says New York Fed

Sources told the FT that the Commerce Department believes current tariffs have affected consumers, raising the prices of products including drink cans and pie tins.

The news comes amid a study from the New York Fed, which found that Americans are paying for 90% of the increased costs from tariffs. The average tariff rate on U.S. imports has risen to 13% from 2.6%, although exporters aren't absorbing those costs. Instead, they have kept prices steady, while importers have passed on higher costs to consumers.

data.title: Is the XRP Crash Over? This Rare On-Chain Signal Is Flashing a Buy for February 2026
data.description:
XRP's price (XRP-USD) is showing signs of strength this Friday as it fights to stay above its daily low of $1.35. After a rough week where the token failed to keep its footing above $1.40, traders are searching for any hint of a turnaround. While the general crypto market feels a bit shaky, specific data points suggest that XRP might be bottoming out. This means the price could be reaching the lowest point of its recent slide before starting a slow climb back up.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On-chain Signal Flashes a "Buy"

One of the most interesting clues comes from a metric called the MVRV-Z Score. This tool helps investors figure out if a coin is cheap or expensive compared to its fair value. Right now, this score has been in the negative zone for nearly two weeks. In the past, whenever this happened, it was usually followed by a massive price jump. For example, back in July 2024, a similar negative score was the starting point for XRP to skyrocket from $0.45 all the way to $2.91 just a few months later.

Exchanges Get Flooded with XRP Tokens

However, it's not all good news. Data shows that the amount of XRP sitting on exchanges has actually been increasing lately, hitting about 12.99 billion tokens. This is usually a sign of caution. When people move their coins onto exchanges, it often means they are getting ready to sell. This extra supply can act like a heavy weight, making it harder for the price to move up even if more people start buying.

Key XRP Price Levels to Watch

For XRP to really start a recovery, it needs to break back above the $1.40 level and stay there. If it can do that, it might clear the path to test higher prices again. But if the selling continues and the price drops below $1.30, the next safety net isn't until $1.25 or even the psychological floor of $1.00.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.3785.
data.author: Annika Masrani
